problem.title: Odd Even Linked List
problem.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, group all the nodes with odd indices together followed by the nodes with even indices, and return the reordered list.
assumptions: Preserve relative order within odd and even groups.

example.explanation: Group odd-indexed nodes (1,3,5) followed by even-indexed (2,4). In-place, O(1) space.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain two pointers — one advancing through odd-indexed nodes and one through even-indexed nodes — weaving them apart in a single pass. Save the head of the even list, then after the loop reconnect the tail of the odd list to that saved even head. The key insight is that both pointers always move two steps at a time, so the relative order within each group is preserved automatically.
solutions.code_python:
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0): self.val=val; self.next=None
from typing import Optional

def odd_even_list(head: Optional['ListNode']) -> Optional['ListNode']:
    if not head or not head.next:
        return head
    odd = head
    even = head.next
    even_head = even
    while even and even.next:
        odd.next = even.next
        odd = odd.next
        even.next = odd.next
        even = even.next
    odd.next = even_head
    return head
